title: Prom Threat: Carrie and the Uneasy Legacy Of the Outcast Queen
description: Can Carrie White simultaneously be the hero and the monster? The clues lie in what has been jettisoned in an otherwise faithful adaptation from page to screen. By removing the conventional protagonist, the adaptation takes on ominous new implications and truly becomes a Brian De Palma film.
hook: De Palma's Carrie flips the script by making the outcast the actual monster—what gets lost when you strip away the book's moral complexity.

title: America’s Greatest Food Export
description: Ranch dressing has become, deservedly or not, a face of the nation during the World Cup.
hook: Ranch dressing conquered America and now the world. A condiment's rise tells us something unsettling about what we export as culture.

title: Andrew Sean Greer Refuses to Kill His Darlings
description:
Like so many others (including the Pulitzer Prize committee), I read Andrew Sean Greer’s Less in 2017 and was endlessly charmed by the singularity of Greer’s voice. Rollicking through vivid landscapes and madcap scenarios, Greer’s novels commit to comedy and eccentricity, standing out in an often muted literary landscape. His newest book, Villa Coco, set […]

The post Andrew Sean Greer Refuses
hook: Andrew Sean Greer writes comedies so precise they feel reckless—eccentric characters that somehow reveal deeper truths about desire and reinvention.

title: How the Crossword Conquered America
description: "Even in a boom time for games, the decidedly old-fashioned puzzles are more popular than ever. I went to the best place on Earth to understand why."
hook: Crosswords are booming while everything else is supposed to be dying. A reporter discovers why this anachronistic puzzle still grips millions.

title: 4K UHD Blu-ray Review: John Waters’s ‘Desperate Living’ on the Criterion Collection
description:
Desperate Living is Waters’s most furious political statement.

The post 4K UHD Blu-ray Review: John Waters’s ‘Desperate Living’ on the Criterion Collection
hook: John Waters's "Desperate Living" is his rawest political fury—a repulsive film that forces you to confront why you're laughing at it.
